Course Title: AI in Structural Design: Hidden Assumption Risks
Category: Infrastructure & Construction: Construction, Engineering, Building Materials, Real Estate Development
Who is this for: Structural engineers, civil engineers, design engineers, construction managers, project managers, architects, building inspectors, engineering consultants
Team or Dept this is for: Engineering, Construction, Project Management, Design, Quality Assurance, Risk Management
Description: When AI-generated designs overlook critical engineering constraints.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with structural engineering principles and some exposure to AI tools used in design or construction workflows will help you get the most from this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the hidden assumptions embedded in AI-generated structural designs and explain how these assumptions can introduce critical engineering risks in real-world construction projects. You will also apply practical evaluation techniques to assess AI design outputs, ensuring that fundamental structural constraints are recognised, questioned, and properly validated before any design progresses to implementation.
